title: kernel.visual_debug.dom_snapshot IPC (DOM JSON via CDP)
goal: Per spec §6.4.3 (AC-VISUAL-DEBUGGER-IPC) implement `kernel.visual_debug.dom_snapshot(scope)` returning a DOM JSON snapshot via CDP DOM.getDocument / DOM.describeNode. Used by the Diagnostics panel (cluster X) to render collapsible DOM trees with stable-element-id highlighting.
expected_diff_shape: New Tauri command in app/src-tauri/src/visual_debug.rs (MT-018); cdp_client.rs extension for DOM endpoints; test asserting the returned JSON has expected schema.
unblock_conditions: MT-018 CDP wiring + MT-021 driver scaffold so CDP endpoints can be tested via Playwright connect.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.4.3. Extend cdp_client.rs with `dom_snapshot(scope: DomScope) -> DomTree` calling CDP `DOM.getDocument { depth: -1, pierce: true }` (full traversal incl. shadow roots). DomTree schema: `{ root: DomNode }` where `DomNode { node_id: i64, node_name: String, node_type: u8, attributes: BTreeMap<String,String>, children: Vec<DomNode>, stable_element_id: Option<String> (extracted from data-testid attribute if present) }`. The stable_element_id extraction supports HBR-VIS-001's stable-element-id selector pattern. scope = { Full, Selector(String) } where Selector calls `DOM.querySelector` first to scope the snapshot to a subtree. Tauri command: `#[tauri::command] async fn visual_debug_dom_snapshot(scope: DomScope) -> Result<DomTree, String>`. The Tauri command is added to the manual via MT-013 paired-diff enforcement (HBR-MAN-001 will block the commit if missing). Tests: connect via MT-021 driver, call dom_snapshot(Full), assert returned tree has root.node_name == "#document" and at least one descendant with non-empty stable_element_id (assumes the app's main page has at least one data-testid). Negative: dom_snapshot(Selector("#nonexistent")) returns Err.
risk_if_missed: Without DOM snapshot IPC, the Diagnostics panel cannot render DOM trees; HBR-VIS-001 stable-element-id selector pattern has no surface.
validator_focus: DOM tree serializes cleanly to JSON; stable_element_id extraction is from data-testid attribute (not heuristic); ManualEntry exists for the new IPC channel per HBR-MAN-001 paired-diff.

minimum_controls: stable_element_id sources only from data-testid attribute (no heuristic fallback that could be wrong).
Tauri command landing triggers HBR-MAN-001 paired-diff (manual must update in same commit).
Selector-scoped snapshot returns Err on missing element (not silent empty tree).
